sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 15.9 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.15)

statement.latex: An automorphism$$of the free group$F_n$on the free generators$x_1, x_2, , x_n$is called conjugating\/ if$x_i^{ }= t_i^ {-1} x_ { (i)} t_i$,$i=1,\, 2, ,n$, for some permutation${ S}_n$and some elements$t_i F_n$. The set of conjugating automorphisms fixing the product$x_1x_2 x_n$forms the braid group$B_n$. The group$B_n$is linear for any$n 2$, while the group of all automorphisms${ Aut}\, F_n$is not...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
